channel.title: Intension Updates
channel.description: Most recent updates for Intension macOS app.

item.title: Version 0.36.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.36.0

  • Smarter sessions — The timeline no longer splits your day into rigid 30-minute blocks. Intension now detects when you actually switch tasks — based on what apps you're using, how long you've been idle, and whether you're in a meeting — and creates sessions that match what you were really doing. All of this runs entirely on your device.
  • Live session indicator — Your current session now appears in the timeline as it happens.
  • Stability and polish — Fixed a handful of rough edges: system processes no longer appear in your top apps, the session list scrolls more smoothly, confidence indicators display correctly, and duplicate model downloads are prevented.

item.title: Version 0.35.2
item.description:
What's New in 0.35.2

  • Smarter session labels — Sessions now correctly identify what you're actually doing — coding, design, communication, research — instead of most things showing up as "meeting." The app recognizes far more apps out of the box and uses a faster rule-based classifier before falling back to on-device ML.
  • New timeline interaction — Replaced the old split-view with a single smooth scroll surface. Scroll up from the attention hero to browse your full session history. Tighter hero layout with centered numerals and a cleaner attention detail card.

item.title: Version 0.35.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.35.1

  • Seconds front and center — The hero metric now shows your attention span in seconds as the primary number, with your state (Sustained, Engaged, etc.) as a badge underneath. Bigger, clearer, more useful at a glance.
  • Confidence-aware display — Intension now knows how confident it is in your attention span. When data is sparse you'll see "Measuring…", then a range like "27–45s", then a precise number as confidence builds. No more showing a number it isn't sure about.
  • Tap the chart to explore — Tap any point on the attention chart and the session list scrolls to that moment. Works across Today, 7-day, and 30-day views.
  • Updated menu bar — The menu bar now shows seconds with confidence display and uses the Intension logo instead of the animated orb.

item.title: Version 0.35.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.35.0

  • Tap to explore your best session — The "Longest Focus" metric and the best-focus dot on your 7-day and 30-day charts are now tappable. Tap either one and the timeline scrolls to that session and expands it, so you can see what you were doing during your best work.
  • Cleaner Today view — Removed the trending text from the Today view. Trend comparisons need multiple days of context, so Today now stays focused on what's happening right now.
  • Session cards show attention quality — Session cards are now tinted by attention level, matching the color language of the hero metric so your best sessions stand out visually.
  • System apps filtered out — Background processes like loginwindow no longer appear as phantom multi-hour focus sessions. The "Best Session" metric now only reflects real work.
  • Faster, fewer hangs — Fixed 5 reported app-hang issues. The animated logo loads off the main thread, app icons are cached after first load, and system API calls are time-bounded to prevent stalls.
  • 9 QA fixes — Improved data accuracy for session durations, chart hover tooltips, and attention score reliability.

item.title: Version 0.34.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.34.1

Attention you can read

The hero metric now classifies your attention into four states — Sustained, Engaged, Passive, and Idle — instead of raw seconds. Shows up in both the main view and the menu bar.

The classifier watches keystroke density and app-switching frequency over a 5-minute window. Brief breaks won't knock you down — upgrades are instant, downgrades wait.

  • Categorical Attention — Sustained / Engaged / Passive / Idle replaces the seconds-based hero. Same underlying data, translated into something you can read at a glance.
  • State Explainers — Each state has a one-liner description and an info sheet explaining what Intension detects, how states transition, and how the system behaves.
  • Smarter Classification — Keystroke density + app-switch frequency over a rolling 5-minute window. Asymmetric holdoff: upgrades instantly, downgrades wait 3 minutes.
  • Updated Copy — Sub-metric labels, trend indicators, and info sheets rewritten to describe signals without projecting behavior. Trend-down is blue, never red.
  • Readable Info UI — Explanation text bumped to 13pt for Apple HIG compliance.
  • Analytics Foundation — Tracking events for info button taps, session toggles, and metric detail views.
  • Cleanup — Removed dead trend code, fixed font inconsistencies, quieted debug logging.

item.title: Version 0.34.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.34.0

  • Session Timeline — New vertical session list helps you understand your day. Expandable cards showing app-switching activity, icons, and durations all make your data more accessible
  • Chart Gaps — The Today view now shows gaps in data collection, from say sleep, as a dotted line. Makes it clear when you were on your devices.
  • Metric Explanations — Do you want to know what the numbers mean, how to change them, and if its good or bad? Tap the new info icon to learn more!
  • Setup — A new setting to launch Intension on bootup. Branded install files and links.
  • Performance — Smoother UI with fewer freezes, more accurate attention scores and session detection.

item.title: Version 0.33.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.33.1

  • Full Disk Access setup guide — New animated step-by-step guide walks you through granting Full Disk Access in System Settings, required on macOS 26.
  • Menu bar stays visible on crowded menu bars — When macOS hides the menu bar item due to limited space (e.g., on notched MacBooks), Intension now automatically switches to a compact icon-only mode so it stays visible.
  • Today chart uses 24-hour window — The Today view now shows hourly data from the last 24 hours instead of calendar-today, so the chart isn't empty right after midnight.
  • Consistent trend directions — Fixed an issue where the attention trend arrow could point in different directions on different screens for the same data.
  • ML inference no longer gets stuck — Added a 60-second timeout to on-device AI generation. Previously, a hung model could permanently block all ML-powered features until app restart.
  • Fixed two app crashes — Resolved a crash when loading the ML model simultaneously from multiple code paths, and a 2-second freeze when opening Settings (file size calculations now run in the background).
  • Fixed another app freeze — Calendar timezone lookups that blocked the main thread during daylight saving transitions are now handled off-thread.
  • Smarter model prewarming — The ML model prewarmer now checks if inference is already running before loading, avoiding GPU contention.
  • ML model cache recovery — If macOS purges the model cache, Intension detects it and re-downloads automatically instead of showing errors.

item.title: Version 0.33.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.33.0

  • Onboarding redesign — Completely redesigned 5-screen onboarding to match the new Figma spec: dark gradient background, per-step CTA colors, glass morphism cards, new progress bar with colored dots, and a 6-tier attention color scale.
  • Main screen redesign — Updated the hero attention span display with the new Figma 6-tier color scale (warm coral → cool blue) and cleaner typography.
  • Attention span consistent everywhere — The number in the menu bar, main app, and onboarding first reading now all come from the same source of truth. Previously these could show different values.
  • 5 app hang fixes — Resolved five separate causes of the app freezing for 2+ seconds: cached app name lookups in Screen Time export, moved JSON decoding and data processing off the main thread, reused DateFormatters instead of re-creating per frame, and moved dictionary construction off the UI thread.
  • Removed legacy onboarding — The old 37-screen onboarding has been removed (~14,000 lines). Only the streamlined 5-screen onboarding remains.

item.title: Version 0.32.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.32.0

  • Attention span consistent everywhere — The number in the menu bar, main app, and onboarding first reading now all come from the same source of truth. Previously these could show different values.
  • 5 app hang fixes — Resolved five separate causes of the app freezing for 2+ seconds: cached app name lookups in Screen Time export, moved JSON decoding and data processing off the main thread, reused DateFormatters instead of re-creating per frame, and moved dictionary construction off the UI thread.
  • Removed legacy onboarding — The old 37-screen onboarding has been removed (~14,000 lines). Only the streamlined 5-screen onboarding remains.

item.title: Version 0.31.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.31.1

  • Frosted glass onboarding — The onboarding window now has the same translucent blur as the main app, replacing the flat dark background.
  • New app icon — Fresh icon across all sizes.
  • Chart labels no longer clipped — Y-axis labels on the attention chart (0s, 1:40, etc.) are now fully visible.
  • Time range buttons always work — The 7d/30d/90d selectors are now responsive even when a session detail is open.
  • Better meeting detection — Browser-based meetings (Google Meet, Zoom, Teams in Chrome/Arc/Safari) are now correctly classified instead of being labeled "unfocused research."
  • Fixed inflated attention spans — Session detail no longer shows impossibly high numbers (e.g. 2,046s) for single-window sessions.
  • Readable baseline numbers — The hero attention span is now legible during the 7-day "Building your baseline" phase.
  • Fixed rare Metal crash — Resolved a crash caused by concurrent ML inference exceeding GPU resource limits.
  • Quieter menu bar updates — Attention span in the menu bar now updates every 30 seconds instead of every second, reducing CPU overhead.

item.title: Version 0.31.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.31.0

  • Redesigned onboarding — New guided flow walks you through setup with a personal hook, clear promise, and combined permissions + model download on a single screen. Your first attention reading now uses real historical data instead of a placeholder.
  • Timeline shows the last 12 hours by default — Sessions are larger and easier to read. Labeled time markers every 3 hours ("6 AM", "9 AM", "12 PM") help you orient at a glance.
  • Day boundaries on the timeline — Clear visual gaps between days with date labels ("Tuesday, Mar 4") and session counts so multi-day scrolling makes sense.
  • Sphere colors match the menu bar — Timeline bubbles now use the same dusty red → amber → sage green palette as the menu bar orb, so colors feel consistent across the app.
  • Deep focus glow — Sessions where you held focus above 25 seconds get a subtle outer glow that intensifies with attention depth — your best sessions stand out.
  • Smarter session labels — Fixed an issue where every session was labeled "planning" with identical confidence. Sessions now get varied, accurate labels like coding, research, communication, or meeting.
  • Better meeting detection — Browser-based meetings (Google Meet, Zoom in Chrome) are now correctly identified by window title, not just app name.
  • Cleaner menu bar — Removed app icons from the menu bar for a simpler, less distracting status item. Real-time display now matches timeline metrics.
  • Fixed empty timeline bug — Resolved an issue where the timeline showed no session orbs if the ML model selection was cleared. The app now auto-recovers.
  • Accurate 30d/90d metrics — Long-range metrics now show daily averages instead of cumulative sums, giving you a more meaningful picture of your attention over time.
  • Smoother scrolling — Timeline performance improved dramatically with viewport culling — only visible elements are rendered, eliminating lag and memory spikes on long timelines.
  • Headline stays readable — The attention span number is no longer dimmed during the 7-day baseline period, keeping it legible against the dark background.
  • Fixed app hangs — Resolved three separate causes of 2-second freezes during permission checks and window server calls.
  • Session detail polish — Fixed panel alignment, text clipping, background bleed-through, and duplicate information in session detail popovers.
  • Parallax animation smoothness — The hero/tracker split view now animates at a steady 60fps with no jitter.
  • Onboarding permission fix — Granting Screen Time permission now correctly returns you to onboarding without requiring a double grant.

item.title: Version 0.30.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.30.1

  • Attention metrics explained — Tap Deep Work, Context Switching, or Longest Focus to see what each metric measures and why it matters
  • Smarter trend labels — The chart annotation now shows "Steady" only when your data is genuinely consistent, and "Variable" when there are significant day-to-day swings
  • Timeline sphere colors — Session bubbles now shift from amber (scattered) → teal (moderate) → blue (deep focus) based on your attention span during that session
  • Back navigation from Insights — Added a "← Attention" button at the top of the Shape of Attention view to return to the main attention screen
  • Auto-expand on session tap — Tapping a session sphere while the timeline is minimized now automatically expands it so you can see the full detail
  • Cleaner session detail — Removed tips and insight text from the session detail popover for a focused, distraction-free view
  • Grammar fixes — "1 window tracked" now correctly uses singular/plural form
  • Simplified baseline message — While building your baseline, the app now shows "Building your baseline" without a day counter
  • Removed bottom stats bar — Cleaned up the tracker dashboard by removing the median attention span cards
  • Improved time range picker — The 7d / 30d / 90d buttons are now easier to click with larger tap targets
  • Fixed tracker rendering — Resolved an issue where the timeline section could display incorrectly on some configurations

item.title: Version 0.30.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.30.0

  • Attention Span Dashboard — See your median attention span front and center with a color-coded hero number that shifts from amber to teal as your focus improves
  • Time Range Selector — Switch between 7-day, 30-day, and 90-day views with smooth animated transitions — sliding picker, morphing numbers, and cross-fading charts
  • Attention Trend Chart — Daily sparkline showing your attention span over time with trend direction and change summary
  • Supporting Metrics — Deep work hours, context switching rate, and longest focus session displayed below the chart — tap any metric for details
  • Parallax Split View — Hero stats on top, session timeline on bottom — tap either half to bring it forward with a depth-layered animation
  • Guided First-Run Reveal — Animated count-up introduction on your first visit that walks you through your attention data, skippable anytime
  • New Onboarding Flow — Redesigned 5-step onboarding: understand the problem, accept the invitation, download the ML model, grant permissions, see your first reading
  • Background Model Download — ML model downloads in the background during onboarding so you're never blocked — progress bubble shows on the dashboard if still in progress
  • Menu Bar Attention Orb — Tiny color-shifting orb in the menu bar that reflects your current attention span at a glance
  • Stability & Performance — Fixed timer leaks, eliminated force-unwraps, removed redundant database queries, and resolved task race conditions

item.title: Version 0.29.2
item.description:
What's New in 0.29.2

  • Live Attention Span — Your menu bar now shows how long you typically focus before switching apps, updated every 15 seconds
  • Trend Arrow — See at a glance if your focus is improving ↑, declining ↓, or holding steady →
  • Smarter during deep focus — The reading holds steady when you're locked into one app instead of disappearing

item.title: Version 0.29.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.29.1

  • Better Battery Life — Intension now uses smart caching and debouncing so AI insights don't regenerate unnecessarily, and background tasks run at lower priority to save energy.
  • More Accurate Data — Fixed timezone issues, corrected weekly averages, and filtered out noise so your focus stats, session counts, and screen time are all more reliable.
  • Attention Rating Chart — A new chess.com-style rating chart tracks your attention score over time with a rolling average, so you can see your focus trends at a glance.
  • Clearer Labels & Language — Replaced jargony terms: "Elite" is now "Deep Focus", "Peak Chaos" is "High Switching Period", and archetypes got friendlier names like "Deep Worker" and "Rapid Switcher".
  • Positive Framing — Stats now highlight what you achieved (e.g. "26% Deep Focus") instead of what you didn't (e.g. "74% Shallow Sessions").
  • Simpler Tab Names — Insight tabs are now just "Timeline", "Focus", and "Patterns" — easier to navigate.
  • Recovery Cost Card — A new educational card explains why context switching is costly, backed by research from Gloria Mark.
  • Data Source Badges — Each insight now shows whether it's powered by Intension tracking, Screen Time, or both.
  • Improved Charts — Heatmaps have a color legend, fragmentation uses clearer stacked bars, and dual-axis charts are properly normalized so comparisons aren't misleading.
  • 60+ New App Icons & Names — Added recognition for popular apps like Arc, Raycast, JetBrains IDEs, 1Password, Bear, Craft, Signal, and more.
  • Smarter AI Insights — AI-generated text is now shorter (2 sentences max), avoids prescriptive advice, and timestamps adapt to your 12h/24h clock preference.
  • Late Night Stats Fixed — The late night usage card now shows your daily average instead of a cumulative total across the whole range.
  • Compact Menu Bar Timer — The menu bar timer is back to its compact format ("5m", "1h 2m") instead of showing extra seconds.
  • Duplicate Window Prevention — Opening Intension no longer accidentally creates a second window.

item.title: Version 0.29.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.29.0

  • Accurate Attention Span: The median attention span now reflects how long you actually stay on a single window or tab before switching — no more inflated numbers.
  • Smarter Data Sources: Your dashboard uses real-time window tracking, while Insights pulls from your full device activity history for richer, longer-term trends.
  • Session-Level Detail: Each focus session on the timeline now shows its own median attention span, so you can see which sessions had your deepest focus.

item.title: Version 0.28.2
item.description:
What's New in 0.28.2

  • Dramatically reduced CPU, GPU, and memory usage by keeping the ML model warm between classifications instead of reloading 4.4GB every 5 minutes
  • Fixed unnecessary AI reclassifications running even when nothing changed — now only re-classifies when your activity actually shifts
  • Eliminated a burst of rapid retry attempts after waking from sleep that caused temporary high CPU usage
  • AI inference now pauses when your Mac is thermally throttled or on the lock screen, preserving battery life

item.title: Version 0.28.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.28.1

  • Optimized session classification prompt for Qwen3 models — lower temperature, restructured prompt, cut from ~1,200 to ~550 tokens

item.title: Version 0.28.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.28.0

  • New "Right Now" portal — always-on-top floating blob that shows what you're doing in real time (e.g., CODING, RESEARCH, WRITING)
  • Cmd+I hotkey toggles the portal on/off with debounce protection
  • Three display modes: assertion (high confidence), question (low confidence), and correction (user feedback)
  • Organic morphing blob UI with multi-layer glow, backdrop blur, and amber pulse animation
  • Auto-dismiss after 8s (high confidence) or 12s (medium confidence)
  • Pre-classification runs 10s after session start to eliminate perceived ML latency
  • Classification refreshes every 5 minutes via existing session reclassification timer
  • Live session duration display (computed from session start time, not stale ML output)
  • Session detection with automatic boundaries on app switches (>2 min) and return detection (>10 min away)
  • Meeting detection combining calendar events and 60+ known meeting app bundle IDs
  • User corrections feed back into future classifications via learning loop
  • New GRDB tables for activity sessions and session corrections with 365-day retention
  • Shared SessionClassificationContextBuilder for consistent context assembly
  • Dashboard session sphere rendering simplified to use deterministic FocusSessionComputer output
  • Portal window recreated on each show to prevent blank re-display bug
  • Comprehensive test suite: RightNowInteractor, Presenter, Coordinator, SessionDetector, and MeetingDetection tests

item.title: Version 0.27.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.27.1

  • 🌐 Extract page URLs from browser windows via Accessibility API (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Arc, and more)
  • 🎥 Detect browser-based meetings (Google Meet, Zoom, Teams, Webex) from window titles — video calls no longer penalized for low keystrokes
  • 🧭 Browser library expanded from 7 to 21 — added Opera, Vivaldi, Orion, DuckDuckGo, Zen, Tor, Waterfox, Sidekick, and more
  • ⚡ App icon caching in dashboard for faster rendering

item.title: Version 0.27.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.27.0

  • Faster, more reliable data storage — your data now lives in a proper database instead of loose files
  • Smooth upgrade experience — existing users see a progress screen while their data is automatically migrated (only happens once)
  • Timeline works immediately after upgrade — no need to restart the app to see your history
  • Better error recovery — if something goes wrong with the database, the app self-heals instead of crashing
  • Smaller, cleaner codebase — removed over 1,100 lines of old code that was no longer needed

item.title: Version 0.26.2
item.description:
What's New in 0.26.2

  • Added Settings window accessible from the menu bar (⌘,)
  • Fixed crash when reopening Settings or ML Test Bench windows
  • Clarified metric labels: Score → Attention Score, Most Productive → Today's Best Session
  • Updated ML prompts to score meetings and media fairly
  • Switched time display to 12-hour format

item.title: Version 0.26.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.26.1

  • Switched all dashboard times to 12-hour AM/PM format (e.g. 2:30 PM instead of 14:30)
  • Session labels with embedded times are now also displayed in 12-hour format
  • Timeline time ranges, peak hours, and activity timestamps all use the new format

item.title: Version 0.26.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.26.0

  • Horizontal session timeline with interactive spheres sized by duration
  • Click-to-expand session detail popover with activity blocks and efficiency score
  • Live focus sessions computed in real-time before daily recap
  • Session start dots marking tracking resumption after breaks
  • Two-finger trackpad scrolling on timeline
  • Visible range stats cards (efficiency, peak hour, active hours)
  • Fixed session popover showing wrong activity windows
  • Removed floating window and mood feedback prompt

item.title: Version 0.25.2
item.description:
What's New in 0.25.2

  • Fixed race conditions in voice recording and window tracking for improved stability
  • Fixed duplicate windows appearing on app relaunch
  • Improved sleep/wake handling — app recovers gracefully after Mac wakes up
  • ML model automatically unloads under memory pressure to keep your Mac responsive
  • Added Sentry Metrics for performance monitoring across different Mac hardware (M1–M4)
  • Full ML model lifecycle tracking — download, load, inference, and unload timing
  • System health metrics (CPU, GPU, memory, launch time) reported daily via MetricKit
  • Removed old unused Dashboard code, replaced by the current Activity Tracker
  • Fixed Python engine dropping responses during concurrent requests
  • Improved error handling — removed force unwraps in data retention and storage
  • Reduced battery impact from tab tracking by filtering noisy accessibility events

item.title: Version 0.25.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.25.1

  • Smarter Focus Sessions – Session timestamps are now computed precisely in code, eliminating AI-generated time errors
  • Better App Classification – Terminal, iTerm, and Warp now correctly categorized as development tools
  • Improved Daily Insights – More accurate efficiency scoring with proper temperature tuning
  • Sentry Release Tracking – Crash reports now properly linked to app versions for faster debugging

item.title: Version 0.25.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.25.0

  • Fixed Gemma models not working for AI inference
  • Fixed memory building up during AI processing
  • Fixed ML Test Bench not releasing memory after tests complete
  • Reduced peak memory usage during AI tasks
  • Improved memory cleanup after each AI generation

item.title: Version 0.24.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.24.0

  • Faster AI Insights - Completely redesigned on-device AI for faster, more reliable productivity analysis
  • ML Test Bench - New developer tool for testing and comparing AI model performance (accessible via menu bar)
  • Improved Reliability - AI-generated insights now always produce valid results without retries
  • Performance Tracking - Behind-the-scenes monitoring to help us improve AI speed on different Mac models
  • Simplified Settings - Cleaner ML model settings with fewer options to configure
  • Bug Fixes - Fixed issues in settings

item.title: Version 0.23.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.23.1

  • Sparkle Fixes: Fixed version comparison to use marketing version, ensuring updates are properly detected
  • Auto-Updates: Added "Check for Updates" menu item and enabled automatic update checks
  • Crash Reporting: dSYMs now uploaded to Sentry for better crash symbolication

item.title: Version 0.23.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.23.0

  • Sparkle Fixes: Fixed version comparison to use marketing version, ensuring updates are properly detected
  • Auto-Updates: Added "Check for Updates" menu item and enabled automatic update checks
  • Crash Reporting: dSYMs now uploaded to Sentry for better crash symbolication

item.title: Version 0.22.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.22.1

See release notes on GitHub for details.
